economic_notes: First Nation has outstanding claims against Government of Canada: a reserve creation claim and a treaty benefits claim, financed through RBC bank loans with insurance coverage. Waterhen Forestry Products LP is a government business enterprise accounted for by modified equity method with total revenue of $5,843,240. COVID-19 impacted operations but total revenues were not negatively impacted as ISC increased financial resources.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Nation is in Saskatchewan. Two active claims against Government of Canada (reserve creation and treaty benefits) financed through RBC loans totaling ~$2.5M drawn as of March 31, 2021 with insurance backing; costs expensed as incurred ($1,725,396 cumulative). Three consolidated subsidiaries: CMHC Housing Program, Resort North Ltd., Resort South Ltd. One GBE: Waterhen Forestry Products LP (99.99% interest, modified equity method). First Nation not in compliance with timely financial statement submission covenant to First Nations Bank but lender confirmed no acceleration. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC + MLTC Program Services + Meadow Lake Health + MLTC Child & Family + CMHC + Northern Lights CDC = 10,915,854 + 2,013,535 + 3,112,695 + 45,749 + 1,097,496 + 29,000 = 17,214,329; osr = 20,465,169 - 17,214,329 = 3,250,840. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text.
